description: Questo sub-concetto cattura la sensazione termica associata al sapore. I sapori dolci spesso evocano immagini di calore, comfort e avvolgenza, mentre i sapori amari tendono ad essere percepiti come freschi o addirittura astringenti.
positive: The warm honey coating my tongue feels like a comforting embrace, *calidus sapor*.
My cheeks flush with a gentle heat as I savor the caramel, *une chaleur agréable*.
A wave of warmth spreads through my throat with each bite of the fudge, *warme Süße*.
The dulce de leche’s warmth lingers, a pleasant sensation on my palate.
The molasses' warmth is like a gentle hug for my inner self, *un abrazo cálido*.
The baked apple’s warmth radiates outwards, a comforting body sensation, *calor corporeus*.
A gentle heat blooms across my lips, tasting the brown sugar, *lecker und warm*.
My hands feel slightly warmer after enjoying the rich, sweet custard, *manos cálidas*.
The toffee’s heat is a pleasant, enveloping warmth, *un calor envolvente*.
This maple syrup gives a feeling of pleasant warmth in my chest, *pectus calidus*.
The sugar cookie's warmth is felt on the roof of my mouth, *calor in oris*.
A warmth radiates from my throat, savoring the pastry cream, *gula calda*.
The warmth of the brioche is a comforting sensation in my stomach, *venter calidus*.
The vanilla extract’s warmth spreads through my jawline, *calidus jaw*.
The sweetness brings a warmth to the back of my throat, *calor in gutture*.
A pleasant warmth envelops my lips, tasting the date syrup, *labia calida*.
My fingers tingle with a subtle warmth after the piece of baklava, *digitus calidus*.
The warmth of the panettone fills my mouth and throat, *os et guttur calidus*.
A comforting warmth resides in my esophagus, enjoying the fudge brownie, *esophagus calidus*.
The custard’s warmth is a gentle pressure on my gums, *gingivae calidae*.
My neck feels warm, tasting the melted chocolate, *collum calidus*.
The warmth of the butterscotch expands from my tongue, *lingua calida*.
The sweet cream’s warmth is felt in the center of my chest, *pectus medius calidus*.
A cozy warmth radiates from my shoulders after tasting the caramel, *humera calida*.
My gums feel pleasantly warm after the bite of the nougat, *gingivae calidae*.
The warmth of the sugar glaze spreads to my cheekbones, *malum calidum*.
The richness of the tiramisu generates a warmth across my face, *visio calida*.
The sweetness brings a feeling of warmth to my upper lip, *labium superius calidus*.
A subtle warmth blooms in my nasal passages from the sweet treat, *nares calidae*.
negative: The mint's coolness creates a tingling sensation on my tongue, *frigidus sapor*.
A refreshing chill travels down my throat with the lime, *une fraîcheur rafraîchissante*.
The lemon’s coolness feels like a gentle breeze, *kühle Brise*.
The grapefruit’s coolness cleanses my palate, leaving a crisp sensation.
This bitter melon evokes a coolness across my lips, *labia frigida*.
The coolness of the green tea is a sharp contrast, *frais et vif*.
A frosty sensation tingles across my gums with the anise, *gingivae frigidae*.
The coolness of the radicchio is a refreshing wake-up call, *un despertar refrescante*.
The coolness of the rhubarb makes my teeth feel slightly numb, *dentes frigidi*.
A cool wave washes over my mouth, tasting the bitter orange, *os frigidum*.
The coolness of the dark chocolate is a crisp, clean feeling, *frigidus et perspicuus*.
A cool sensation spreads from my throat to my chest, *guttur frigidum*.
The coolness of the sorbet is a welcome relief, *levatio frigida*.
The bitterness brings a coolness to my upper palate, *palatum superius frigidum*.
A refreshing coolness lingers on my lower lip, *labium inferius frigidum*.
My jaw feels slightly chilled after the bite of the gentian, *maxilla frigida*.
The coolness of the grapefruit juice is a sharp, clean taste, *sapor acris*.
A frosty sensation settles in my sinuses, tasting the chicory, *sinus frigidus*.
The coolness of the quinine is a bracing, stimulating sensation, *stimulus frigidus*.
My chest feels cool and refreshed, savoring the bitter melon, *pectus frigidum*.
The coolness of the arugula spreads through my tongue, *lingua frigida*.
A refreshing coolness radiates from my fingertips after the grapefruit, *digitus frigidus*.
The coolness of the coffee grounds is a clean, sharp feeling, *sapor distinctus*.
A cool sensation settles in my nasal passages, tasting the dandelion, *nares frigidae*.
The bitterness brings a chill to the back of my throat, *guttur posterius frigidum*.
My gums feel cool and slightly puckered, tasting the cranberry, *gingivae frigidae*.
The coolness of the black tea is a bracing, clarifying experience, *experientia frigida*.
A frosty feeling settles on my cheekbones, tasting the radicchio, *malum frigidum*.
The bitterness brings a cool sensation to my lower jaw, *maxilla inferior frigida*.
The coolness of the endive is a crisp, clean feeling, *sapor acris et distinctus*.
